context: Lord Vishnu classifies sins into mahapatakas (great sins) and upapatakas (minor sins), detailing specific transgressions such as killing, stealing, dishonesty, and violence, along with their karmic consequences. The chapter emphasizes that even mental sins carry weight.

verses.hindi:
श्रीगरुड उवाच —
पापानां च प्रकारं मे वद विष्णो जगद्गुरो ।
महापातकसंयुक्तमुपपातकमेव च ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Garuḍa uvāca —
Pāpānāṃ ca prakāraṃ me vada Viṣṇo Jagadguro |
mahāpātakasaṃyuktam upapātakam eva ca ||
verses.translation: Garuda said: Tell me, O Vishnu, Teacher of the World, the classification of sins — both the great sins (mahapatakas) and the minor sins (upapatakas).

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
शृणु तार्क्ष्य प्रवक्ष्यामि पापानां विविधां गतिम् ।
महापातकसंयुक्तमुपपातकमेव च ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
Śṛṇu Tārkṣya pravakṣyāmi pāpānāṃ vividhāṃ gatim |
mahāpātakasaṃyuktam upapātakam eva ca ||
verses.translation: The Lord said: Listen, O Tarksya, I shall describe the various consequences of sins, both the great sins and the minor sins.

verses.hindi:
ब्रह्महत्या सुरापानं स्तेयं गुर्वङ्गनागमः ।
महापातकमित्याहुस्तत्संसर्गश्च पञ्चमम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Brahmahatyā surāpānaṃ steyaṃ gurvaṅganāgamaḥ |
mahāpātakam ity āhus tat-saṃsargaśca pañcamam ||
verses.translation: Killing a brahmana, drinking intoxicants, stealing, violating the guru's wife — these are declared as great sins, and association with such sinners is the fifth.

verses.hindi:
ब्रह्महत्यां कृतवते नरकः शाश्वतो भवेत् ।
यक्ष्मा रोगश्च जायेत जन्मजन्मनि पापिनः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Brahmahatyāṃ kṛtavate narakaḥ śāśvato bhavet |
yakṣmā rogaśca jāyeta janmajanmani pāpinaḥ ||
verses.translation: For one who commits the killing of a brahmana, there is eternal hell. The disease of consumption arises in birth after birth for such a sinner.

verses.hindi:
सुरापानरतो मर्त्यो म्लेच्छयोनिं व्रजेद् ध्रुवम् ।
कृमिकीटपतङ्गादिजन्मसु प्रतिपद्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
Surāpānarato martyo mlecchayoniṃ vraje dhruvam |
kṛmikīṭapataṅgādi-janmasu pratipadyate ||
verses.translation: A mortal addicted to liquor assuredly falls into degraded births. He takes birth among worms, insects, moths, and the like.

verses.hindi:
सुवर्णस्तेयकृद् विप्रात् नखकेशेषु जायते ।
कुनखी विद्रुमो जन्तुर्दरिद्रो व्याधिपीडितः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Suvarṇasteyakṛd viprāt nakhakeśeṣu jāyate |
kunakhī vidrumo jantur daridro vyādhipīḍitaḥ ||
verses.translation: One who steals gold from a brahmana is born with deformed nails and hair. The being becomes wretched, destitute, and afflicted with disease.

verses.hindi:
गुर्वङ्गनागमनकृत् तृणगुल्मलतादिषु ।
स्थावरेषु च जायेत सर्पयोनिं च गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
Gurvaṅganāgamanakṛt tṛṇagulmalatādiṣu |
sthāvareṣu ca jāyeta sarpayoniṃ ca gacchati ||
verses.translation: One who violates the guru's wife is born among grasses, shrubs, and creepers — among immovable things — and takes birth as a serpent.

verses.hindi:
उपपातकमाख्यातं शृणु तार्क्ष्य वदामि ते ।
गोवधः शरणागतत्यागो मित्रद्रोहस्तथैव च ॥
verses.transliteration:
Upapātakam ākhyātaṃ śṛṇu Tārkṣya vadāmi te |
govadhaḥ śaraṇāgatatyāgo mitradrohas tathaiva ca ||
verses.translation: Now hear about the minor sins, O Tarksya, I shall tell you. Cow-slaughter, abandoning those who seek refuge, and betrayal of a friend.

verses.hindi:
वेदनिन्दा देवनिन्दा परनिन्दा तथैव च ।
परक्षेत्रविनाशश्च उपपातकमुच्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
Vedanindā devanindā paranindā tathaiva ca |
parakṣetravināśaśca upapātakam ucyate ||
verses.translation: Reviling the Vedas, blaspheming the gods, slandering others, and destroying another's field — these are called minor sins.

verses.hindi:
कन्यावि क्रयकर्ता च आत्मविक्रयकारकः ।
परिव्राट् परिवित्तिश्च उपपातकमुच्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
Kanyāvikrayakartā ca ātmavikrayakārakaḥ |
parivrāṭ parivittiśca upapātakam ucyate ||
verses.translation: Selling a daughter, selling oneself, an ascetic who breaks his vows, and an elder brother who marries before his younger — these are called minor sins.

verses.hindi:
हिंसा चैव तथा स्तेयं पिशुनत्वं तथैव च ।
क्रोधलोभमदद्वेषा देहजानि तु पातकम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Hiṃsā caiva tathā steyaṃ piśunatvaṃ tathaiva ca |
krodhalobhamadādveṣā dehajāni tu pātakam ||
verses.translation: Violence, theft, tale-bearing, anger, greed, pride, and hatred — these are the sins born of the body.

verses.hindi:
परद्रव्येष्वभिध्यानं मनसा चानिष्टचिन्तनम् ।
वितथाभिनिवेशश्च मनसा त्रिविधं स्मृतम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Paradravyeṣv abhidhyānaṃ manasā cāniṣṭacintanam |
vitathābhiniveśaśca manasā trividhaṃ smṛtam ||
verses.translation: Coveting others' possessions, wishing ill upon others in the mind, and attachment to false doctrines — these three are known as sins of the mind.

verses.hindi:
पारुष्यमनृतं चैव पैशुन्यं चापि सर्वशः ।
असम्बद्धप्रलापश्च वाचा पापचतुष्टयम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Pāruṣyam anṛtaṃ caiva paiśunyaṃ cāpi sarvaśaḥ |
asambaddhapralāpaśca vācā pāpacatuṣṭayam ||
verses.translation: Harsh speech, falsehood, slander in all forms, and senseless prattle — these are the four sins of speech.

verses.hindi:
अदत्तानामुपादानं हिंसा चैवाविधानतः ।
परदारोपसेवा च कायजं त्रिविधं स्मृतम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Adattānām upādānaṃ hiṃsā caivāvidhānataḥ |
paradāropasevā ca kāyajaṃ trividhaṃ smṛtam ||
verses.translation: Taking what is not given, unauthorized violence, and consorting with another's wife — these three are known as sins of the body.

verses.hindi:
अन्नदानविहीनो यः क्षुधितं नैव भोजयेत् ।
तृषितं न च पाययेत् स याति नरकं ध्रुवम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Annadānavihīno yaḥ kṣudhitaṃ naiva bhojayet |
tṛṣitaṃ na ca pāyayet sa yāti narakaṃ dhruvam ||
verses.translation: One who withholds food and does not feed the hungry, who does not give water to the thirsty — that person assuredly goes to hell.

verses.hindi:
विश्वासघातकृद् यस्तु मित्रं हत्वा धनं हरेत् ।
कूपे वापी तडागे च विषं दद्यात् स पातकी ॥
verses.transliteration:
Viśvāsaghātakṛd yastu mitraṃ hatvā dhanaṃ haret |
kūpe vāpī taḍāge ca viṣaṃ dadyāt sa pātakī ||
verses.translation: One who betrays trust, kills a friend for wealth, or puts poison in a well, tank, or pond — that person is a great sinner.

verses.hindi:
असत्यवादी यो मर्त्यः सदा मिथ्या वदेन्नरः ।
कूर्मयोनिं व्रजत्येव दशजन्म सहस्रशः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Asatyavādī yo martyaḥ sadā mithyā vaden naraḥ |
kūrmayoniṃ vrajaty eva daśajanma sahasraśaḥ ||
verses.translation: The mortal who always speaks falsehood takes birth as a tortoise through tens of thousands of births.

verses.hindi:
परीवादं करोति यो नित्यं गुणदोषवित् ।
श्वयोनिं स व्रजत्येव दुर्गन्धो जायते सदा ॥
verses.transliteration:
Parīvādaṃ karoti yo nityaṃ guṇadoṣavit |
śvayoniṃ sa vrajaty eva durgandho jāyate sadā ||
verses.translation: One who constantly engages in slander, finding fault in others' virtues, takes birth as a dog and is always foul-smelling.

verses.hindi:
परदारापहारी यो बलात्कारेण सेवते ।
स सर्पयोनिं लभते अन्धकूपे च पच्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
Paradārāpahārī yo balātkāreṇa sevate |
sa sarpayoniṃ labhate andhakūpe ca pacyate ||
verses.translation: One who forcibly violates another's wife takes birth as a serpent and is scorched in the Dark Well.

verses.hindi:
दीनानाथविहीनानां यः सम्पत्तिं हरेद् बलात् ।
गृध्रयोनिं स लभते दरिद्रो जायते ध्रुवम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Dīnānāthavihīnānāṃ yaḥ sampattiṃ hared balāt |
gṛdhrayoniṃ sa labhate daridro jāyate dhruvam ||
verses.translation: One who forcibly seizes the possessions of the poor, helpless, and destitute takes birth as a vulture and assuredly becomes impoverished.

verses.hindi:
देवद्रव्यं हरेद्यस्तु ब्राह्मणस्वं तथैव च ।
भस्मकुष्ठी भवेन्मर्त्यो रोगी जन्मसहस्रशः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Devadravyaṃ hared yastu brāhmaṇasvaṃ tathaiva ca |
bhasmakuṣṭhī bhaven martyo rogī janmasahasraśaḥ ||
verses.translation: One who steals the property of gods or brahmanas becomes afflicted with leprosy and remains diseased for thousands of births.

verses.hindi:
वृक्षच्छेदे तृणच्छेदे जीवहिंसा प्रजायते ।
जलचारिवधे चैव पापं पापस्य कारणम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Vṛkṣacchede tṛṇacchede jīvahiṃsā prajāyate |
jalacārivadhe caiva pāpaṃ pāpasya kāraṇam ||
verses.translation: In cutting trees and grasses, violence to living beings occurs. In killing aquatic creatures too, sin is the cause of further sin.

verses.hindi:
गर्भपातकरो यस्तु स्त्रीणां दोषेण लिप्यते ।
महापातकमित्याहुः प्रेतत्वं च स गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
Garbhapātakaro yastu strīṇāṃ doṣeṇa lipyate |
mahāpātakam ity āhuḥ pretatvaṃ ca sa gacchati ||
verses.translation: One who causes abortion becomes stained with the sin against women. This is declared a great sin, and such a person attains the state of a ghost.

verses.hindi:
तस्मात् सर्वप्रयत्नेन पापकर्म विवर्जयेत् ।
मनोवाक्कायकर्माणि शुद्धानि परिपालयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Tasmāt sarvaprayatnena pāpakarma vivarjayet |
manovākkāyakarmāṇi śuddhāni paripālayet ||
verses.translation: Therefore with every effort one should avoid sinful deeds. One should keep the actions of mind, speech, and body pure.

verses.hindi:
प्रायश्चित्तं तु कुर्वीत पापे कृते विचक्षणः ।
दानतपोव्रतैः शुद्धिं लभते पापकर्मणाम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Prāyaścittaṃ tu kurvīta pāpe kṛte vicakṣaṇaḥ |
dānatapovrataiḥ śuddhiṃ labhate pāpakarmaṇām ||
verses.translation: A wise person should perform atonement when sin has been committed. Through charity, austerity, and vows, one obtains purification from sinful deeds.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
इति ते कथितं तार्क्ष्य पापानां विविधा गतिः ।
श्रवणात् पठनात् चैव पापेभ्यो मुच्यते नरः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
Iti te kathitaṃ Tārkṣya pāpānāṃ vividhā gatiḥ |
śravaṇāt paṭhanāt caiva pāpebhyo mucyate naraḥ ||
verses.translation: The Lord said: Thus I have told you, O Tarksya, the various consequences of sins. By hearing and reciting this, a person is freed from sins.
